note: Phase A: FCC (2 branches), Master Nursing Care (3 branches), Rebina Sunrise (3 branches), Green Acres (3 branches). Phase B: Gentle Hill Elder Care (1 standalone). All Johor. Operator websites blocked by network policy — editorials based on sheet data, meta descriptions, and GROUPS registry. Data quality: 'full' where sheet row available; 'stub' where only meta description available.

facilities.title: FCC Family Care Centre Johor Bahru
facilities.editorial_summary:
FCC Family Care Centre Johor Bahru operates from Jalan Resam in Taman Bukit Tiram, Ulu Tiram — a residential neighbourhood in eastern Johor Bahru, roughly 20 km from the city centre. The facility is one of two registered entities sharing the same campus under the FCC Family Care Centre group, which positions the development as an integrated senior living complex combining nursing home care, day care, assisted living, and rehabilitation under one roof. The operating entity runs under the familycarecentre.co web presence.

The Ulu Tiram campus serves residents across a range of care levels and fee tiers. Monthly fees in our records run from RM 600/month for shared accommodation up to RM 4,300/month for a private room; a day care option is also available for families not yet ready for full residential placement. The facility lists nursing care, dementia support, physiotherapy, regular doctor visits, transport assistance, and group activities among its included services. A registered nurse is on duty, and visiting doctor rounds are confirmed in the facility's data.

With 137 Google reviews averaging 4.9 stars, FCC JB is one of the most-reviewed nursing homes in Johor — a high review volume that typically reflects sustained family engagement over many admissions rather than a handful of one-off submissions. On a call or viewing, clarify which fee tier applies to which room type and care level, confirm the current RN coverage schedule, ask about dementia unit arrangements, and verify the JKM licence number. The day care option is worth exploring for families working through a step-care transition.

facilities.title: FCC Family Care Centre
facilities.editorial_summary:
FCC Family Care Centre is the flagship registered entity of the FCC group in Johor Bahru, sharing a campus with the separately listed FCC Family Care Centre Johor Bahru in Taman Bukit Tiram, Ulu Tiram. The group presents both entities as part of an integrated senior living and wellness development, with this listing carrying a starting rate of RM 3,500/month — reflecting the premium residential nursing care tier within the campus. The facility's web presence is at familycarecentre.co.

As part of a multi-service campus, the facility covers the full range of care settings the FCC group is known for: nursing home care, assisted living, day care, rehabilitation services, nursing support, physiotherapy, and regular doctor visits. Because this entity listing carries less data than the companion JB listing in our records, families should use the combined Google Maps profile and the familycarecentre.co website for the most current detail on room types, availability, and monthly fees. The starting rate of RM 3,500/month reflects the standard nursing home residential tier; day care and assisted living options may carry different fee structures.

The FCC group's campus has accumulated significant community feedback primarily under the companion JB entity listing — families can use those reviews as a reasonable proxy for the shared campus standard. When enquiring about this flagship entity, ask for the specific JKM licence number attached to this registration, confirm whether pricing is all-inclusive of meals and nursing care, request a full written fee schedule showing all room types, and clarify the process for transitioning between care tiers on the same campus as a resident's needs change over time.

facilities.title: Master Care Centre
facilities.editorial_summary:
Master Care Centre (MCC) is the founding branch of the Master Care Centre group (慈康护理中心), a private nursing care operator headquartered in Johor Bahru with a track record spanning more than a decade. This main branch serves as the anchor for a three-branch network that also includes MCC@Daya and MCC@Abad, all within Johor Bahru. The facility provides nursing home and assisted living care, serving residents who need 24-hour nursing oversight alongside those who require a lighter level of daily assistance.

The group operates across all three branches on a shared model of 24-hour nursing care, dementia support, and rehabilitation services. The website masternursingcare.com is the group's primary information point for current service details, room availability, and contact. At the main branch, our records confirm nursing home and assisted living as the core care types. Pricing is not publicly listed — contact the facility for current monthly fee ranges, as rates vary by room type and the resident's care dependency level. Dementia and rehabilitation services are available across the network.

With 21 Google reviews averaging 4.8 stars, the main branch carries a strong rating that is consistent with the group's overall record. As an established multi-branch operator with an active website and more than a decade of operation in Johor Bahru, the group's track record is verifiable. On a visit, confirm the staffing ratio for day and overnight shifts, ask about the dementia care programme specifics, and check the JKM licence number for this branch. Reviewing Google feedback for the Daya branch alongside the main branch gives a more complete picture of the group's care standard across its network.

facilities.title: Master Nursing Care Centre @Daya
facilities.editorial_summary:
Master Care Centre @Daya (MCC@Daya) is one of three branches operated by Master Care Centre (慈康护理中心), a private nursing care group in Johor Bahru with over a decade of operation. MCC@Daya sits in the Daya district of Johor Bahru and is the most actively reviewed branch in the group, with a strong and consistently positive rating on Google. It shares the network's commitment to 24-hour nursing staffing, dementia support, and rehabilitation services, all documented at masternursingcare.com.

Care services at MCC@Daya mirror the group's standard offering across its three branches: nursing home-level oversight, assistance with activities of daily living, dementia-oriented care, and rehabilitative support. The group's website at masternursingcare.com is the most reliable source for current service details, room types, and contact. Pricing is not publicly posted for any branch — request a written fee schedule when visiting, as monthly rates vary by room configuration and the resident's dependency level. Dementia care and rehabilitation are core elements of the group's programme at all three locations.

The Daya branch's review record reflects sustained positive family feedback, which gives it a strong starting point for families evaluating the MCC group. When visiting, ask about the RN-to-resident ratio on the overnight shift, confirm what dementia assessment and care planning looks like in practice, and request the JKM licence details specific to this branch. Families comparing MCC branches are advised to visit at least Daya and the main branch in the same trip — the physical setup and room availability differ between locations despite operating under a shared care model.

facilities.title: Master Care Centre • Taman Abad
facilities.editorial_summary:
Master Care Centre — Taman Abad (MCC@Abad) is the third branch of the Master Care Centre group (慈康护理中心), located in Taman Abad, Johor Bahru. Like its sibling branches (the main MCC and MCC@Daya), MCC@Abad provides 24-hour residential nursing care under the group's shared care philosophy. Taman Abad is a central and accessible neighbourhood in Johor Bahru, and the branch's placement within a residential precinct makes it a practical option for families living in or near the city centre who want proximity to their relative.

The Master Care Centre group operates consistently across all three branches, with 24-hour nursing care, dementia support, and rehabilitation services forming the core programme at each location. The group's web presence at masternursingcare.com is the best source for current service details and room availability. Pricing is not posted publicly for any MCC branch — request a written fee schedule when visiting, and compare rates across the three locations if cost is a primary factor, as fees may vary slightly by site and room configuration.

MCC@Abad has a smaller online profile than the main and Daya branches, which is typical for a third branch in an established local chain rather than a reflection on care quality. Families should ask about RN duty schedules, dementia care capability specific to this location, and the current JKM licence status when visiting. Viewing all three branches before committing is worth the effort, as the physical setup, room layout, and current availability at each location differ. The group's consistent positive ratings at the main and Daya branches provide reasonable context for evaluating the network's overall standard.

facilities.title: Rebina House Care Centre
facilities.editorial_summary:
Rebina House Care Centre is the founding branch of the Rebina Sunrise Elderly Care group (養身之家), established in 2003 in Taman Kebun Teh, Johor Bahru. Operating for over two decades, it is one of Johor Bahru's longer-standing private nursing homes and serves as the anchor for a three-entity group that includes Sunrise Care Centre Sdn Bhd (adjacent on Jalan Keranji) and Rebina Home Care Centre Sdn Bhd in Taman Abad. The group's web presence is at rebinasunrise.com. Care types at this branch include nursing home care and rehabilitation, with services extending to physiotherapy, doctor visits, palliative care support, and organised activities.

Rebina House's care scope reflects a medically grounded model: nursing care is confirmed, as is physiotherapy and palliative support — a combination that positions it for higher-dependency residents who need clinical services alongside daily personal care. The group's long history in Taman Kebun Teh has built institutional knowledge of the neighbourhood's demographic and care patterns. Pricing is not publicly listed — contact rebinasunrise.com or the facility directly for current monthly rates, which will vary by room type and the resident's care needs.

With 17 Google reviews averaging 4.8 stars, Rebina House has a consistently positive record proportionate to its size. As a family-owned group with a real website and a 20-plus year operating history, the JKM licence and care credentials are worth confirming but the group's longevity provides context. When visiting, clarify RN staffing on the overnight shift, ask about the dementia care approach, and check whether the shared care pathway with Sunrise Care Centre (next door) offers any transitions between the two entities as care needs evolve.

facilities.title: Sunrise Care Centre Sdn Bhd
facilities.editorial_summary:
Sunrise Care Centre Sdn Bhd (Co. 567396-U) is the second entity in the Rebina Sunrise Elderly Care group, occupying No. 48 Jalan Keranji in Johor Bahru — directly adjacent to the group's founding Rebina House Care Centre at Nos. 15 and 17 on the same road. Both share the rebinasunrise.com domain and operate under the same family-owned group established in 2003, making Sunrise Care Centre effectively an expansion of Rebina House's residential capacity within the same neighbourhood cluster.

As part of the Rebina Sunrise group, Sunrise Care Centre is expected to offer the same core services as the main Rebina House branch: nursing home care, rehabilitation support, physiotherapy, visiting doctor rounds, and activities. The group was founded to address a shortage of reliable nursing care in Johor Bahru, and Sunrise Care Centre represents a direct expansion of that capacity. Pricing is not published for this entity — contact the group via rebinasunrise.com for current room rates and availability across both Jalan Keranji properties.

Sunrise Care Centre's close physical proximity to Rebina House (same street, adjacent units) means a site visit to one effectively covers both. Families considering the Rebina Sunrise group should plan a combined visit to all properties — Rebina House, Sunrise Care Centre, and the Taman Abad branch — to compare room types and get a sense of total capacity. Ask about the clinical handover process between the two adjacent entities, confirm the JKM licence for this specific entity (separate from Rebina House), and request the fee breakdown for your preferred room configuration.

facilities.title: Rebina Home Care Centre Sdn Bhd
facilities.editorial_summary:
Rebina Home Care Centre Sdn Bhd is the third entity in the Rebina Sunrise Elderly Care group, operating from Taman Abad in Johor Bahru. Unlike the group's two adjacent Jalan Keranji facilities, this branch operates from a separate neighbourhood, giving the group geographic spread across the city. All three Rebina Sunrise entities — Rebina House, Sunrise Care Centre, and this Taman Abad operation — share the rebinasunrise.com web presence and operate under the same family-owned structure founded in 2003.

As the smallest and least-documented of the three Rebina Sunrise entities, the Taman Abad branch's specific care scope and capacity are best verified directly with the group. The Rebina Sunrise brand is built around nursing home care and rehabilitation, with doctor visits and physiotherapy forming part of the standard service offering at the main branch. Families should confirm whether all services available at Rebina House extend to this location, and ask specifically about staffing levels and room types.

For families already considering the Rebina Sunrise group after researching the Jalan Keranji properties, the Taman Abad branch is worth including in a site visit plan — the neighbourhood's accessibility and the group's track record suggest it is a legitimate option, but the thinner online presence means more should be confirmed in person. Request the JKM licence number for this entity specifically (it will differ from the other two entities' licences), ask for a written fee schedule, and clarify whether care transfers between group branches are managed for residents whose needs change over time.

facilities.title: Green Acres Elderly Care Centre
facilities.editorial_summary:
Green Acres Elderly Care Centre is the flagship residential nursing home of Green Acres Residence Care (Group) Sdn. Bhd., a Johor Bahru-based eldercare operator running its services from greenacreshome.com. The group operates three care entities — this residential care centre, a Home Care Centre, and a separate home care service in Kim Teng Park — together covering a spectrum from institutional nursing to home-based care. The flagship care centre offers nursing home and rehabilitation services with confirmed doctor visits and nursing oversight.

Green Acres' residential offering covers nursing home care and rehabilitation, with visiting doctor rounds confirmed in our records. As a group with an active website, families should refer to greenacreshome.com for the most current service descriptions, room availability, and pricing — fees are not posted publicly in our data, so a direct enquiry is necessary. The group's longevity in the Johor Bahru market, combined with a website-backed presence, gives a reasonable foundation for further verification during a visit.

With 4 Google reviews averaging 4.8 stars, the review count is small but consistent, reflecting a newer or more privately operated facility rather than one with high public visibility. The Green Acres group's multi-arm structure — residential, home care centre, and home care — is worth understanding if you anticipate needing different care settings as your family member's needs change. On a visit, ask about the JKM licence number for the residential care centre, confirm the staffing ratio and RN coverage, request the current fee schedule, and enquire whether a care transition pathway exists between the group's residential and home care services.

facilities.title: Green Acres Home Care Centre
facilities.editorial_summary:
Green Acres Home Care Centre is the second entity under Green Acres Residence Care (Group) Sdn. Bhd. in Johor Bahru, operating alongside the flagship Elderly Care Centre and a separate home care service in Kim Teng Park. All three entities share the greenacreshome.com web presence and fall under a group that has been active in the Johor Bahru eldercare market since the 1990s. Despite the word 'home care' in its name, this entity may function as a residential care service rather than a purely in-home operation — the exact care model should be confirmed when you contact the group, as the three Green Acres entities serve different care modes and the naming convention can be ambiguous.

As part of a group with a functional website, families should use greenacreshome.com as the primary information source for current service scope, room types, and pricing. Care scope and fees for the Home Care Centre are not separately detailed in our records — contact the group directly for current availability and a written fee breakdown. The group's multi-arm structure and Johor Bahru track record suggest operational depth, but direct verification remains important for this specific entity before committing.

When enquiring about the Green Acres Home Care Centre, ask specifically which care model this entity uses (in-facility residential, day care, or in-home visits), request the JKM licence number for this entity separately from the flagship care centre, confirm current staffing arrangements and caregiving qualifications, and ask whether a resident can transition from this entity to the flagship residential care centre if care needs escalate. A combined site visit to both the Home Care Centre and the flagship Elderly Care Centre is the most efficient way to understand what the group offers.

facilities.title: Green Acres Home Care
facilities.editorial_summary:
Green Acres Home Care is the third entity under the Green Acres Residence Care (Group) Sdn. Bhd. umbrella in Johor Bahru, operating from Kim Teng Park. According to our records, the group has been providing elderly residential care in Johor Bahru since the 1990s — making this one of the longer-established care operators in the city. All three Green Acres entities share the greenacreshome.com domain. This entity focuses on home care services, making it the community-based arm of a group that otherwise operates residential care facilities.

As the in-home care arm of the Green Acres group, this entity is most relevant for families seeking professional nursing or personal care delivered at the elder's existing home rather than full residential placement. The group's decades of operation give a reliable foundation, but specific services — frequency of visits, scope of nursing tasks, caregiver qualifications — are best confirmed directly with greenacreshome.com. Pricing for home care services is typically structured per visit or per month based on hours, and is not posted publicly in our data.

For families comparing the three Green Acres entities, note that this Kim Teng Park entity provides home care rather than residential placement — it suits families at an earlier stage of care planning or those whose relative can remain at home with professional support. When enquiring, ask about the specific care tasks covered in a standard visit, confirm caregiver qualifications and supervision arrangements, ask about minimum visit frequency, and find out whether the group offers a pathway to residential care at the flagship facility if in-home care becomes insufficient.

facilities.title: Gentle Hill Elder Care
facilities.editorial_summary:
Gentle Hill Elder Care is a community nursing home in Kulai, Johor — a town of roughly 250,000 in the northern Johor Bahru district, about 40 km from the city centre. The facility's website is at gentlehill.my. Care types in our records include long-term nursing and assisted living, with services extending to physiotherapy, regular doctor visits, transport, and dementia care support alongside daily activities. Kulai's location gives the facility a quieter, lower-density setting than most Johor Bahru city nursing homes, which some families find beneficial for residents requiring a calm environment.

Gentle Hill's service scope covers both higher-dependency nursing care and lighter assisted living, making it a practical option for residents across a range of independence levels. Physiotherapy and dementia care are listed among its services, and doctor visits are confirmed. Pricing is not published on the facility's web presence — contact the facility directly for current monthly fee ranges, which will vary based on the room type and the resident's care needs. The Kulai location also means families living in the northern Johor Bahru corridor (Senai, Kulai, Kluang road) will find it significantly closer than city-centre facilities.

With 43 Google reviews averaging 4.9 stars, Gentle Hill has one of the stronger community-level review profiles among Johor nursing homes of its size. The volume and rating together suggest sustained positive family engagement over time. When visiting, confirm the current RN coverage arrangement (including overnight staffing), ask about the dementia care programme specifics, request a written fee schedule and a room tour, and verify the JKM licence number. The location in Kulai is worth considering as a feature rather than a compromise if city-centre access is not a priority for the family.
